title: Poema Morale
summary: CME00070 is the repository copy of a University of Michigan electronic selection from Anna C. Paues's 1907 Anglia article A Newly Discovered Manuscript of the Poema Morale. The encoding transcribes only the edited text on printed pp. 227–237, not Paues's introduction on pp. 217–226. Paues directly transcribed the newly identified copy in Cambridge, Fitzwilliam Museum, MS McClean 123, ff. 115r–120r, and aligned its 335 Poema Morale lines against Richard Morris's 1873 printed edition of the Trinity College Cambridge MS B.14.52 copy; the two opening encoded lines belong to Sinners Beware rather than Poema Morale. No evidence was found that Paues directly consulted the Trinity codex. No item-specific complete McClean 123 image object was found: Biblissima's generic Mirador control explicitly reports no digitized object, while current scholarly records say the manuscript must be seen onsite. The record therefore preserves Fitzwilliam onsite and whole-object reproduction routes instead of inventing facsimile access. The requested reproduction should include boards or covers, spine, pastedowns, endleaves, blanks, every leaf and edges where offered, with the target mapped separately to ff. 115r–120r. A complete public 216-canvas Trinity comparison object is reused by true hard link and mapped to ff. 2r–9v / canvases 13–28 as a modern supporting source. Complete public copies of the Anglia volume, the standalone Paues article, Morris's complete 1873 volume, the 1912 McClean catalogue, Hill's 1977 study, and DIMEV v1.0 are also cached with exact source links.
review_notes: The intellectual work Poema Morale, the distinctive McClean 123 witness version, Paues's article and transcription, the U-M encoding, and the repository XML are separate layers.

Paues identifies T as the Trinity B.14.52 copy printed by Morris in 1873 and gives corresponding T line numbers, but does not establish direct consultation of the codex. Her comparison is therefore routed through Morris's printed edition; the modern Trinity facsimile remains supporting witness evidence.

Paues labels the second manuscript surface f. 115b. The repository XML instead has MILESTONE N="155b"; the printed page image and the surrounding 115a, 116a sequence establish this as an encoding-layer transcription error, not a second folio.

Paues says the manuscript's first two lines are the opening of Sinners Beware and considers either copying error or a deliberately placed motto. DIMEV consequently treats them as a verse-couplet heading before the Poema Morale witness.

The Biblissima page carries generic IIIF and Mirador interface language, but its live control says that there is no digitized object in the selection and supplies no item-specific Presentation manifest. No public McClean 123 facsimile was recorded.

The Trinity bundle contains every one of the provider's 216 published canvases. It includes covers and insides, four flyleaves, manuscript leaves, stubs and ten UV alternates; no spine or edge views are exposed, so complete means complete published inventory rather than proven exhaustive physical-object photography.

The reused Trinity cache has the same device and inode as the existing CME00035 bundle. Its inventory records 214 exact 1800,-width image requests and two native full-image stub requests; sampled ordinary, target, stub and final images match the recorded profiles and dimensions.

The Fitzwilliam personal-study order form permits ordering images for research but says supplied images may not be published or reproduced without prior approval and a licence. Private cache possession therefore would not authorize Git redistribution, print-on-demand, or commercial use.

notes: IDG/@ID, BIBNO and VID all identify CME00070; the file is 31,565 bytes.
TEMPHEAD records validation and proof review on 2016-09-20, not the original encoding date.

entities.label: University of Michigan Corpus of Middle English item CME00070
entities.description: A public electronic transcription of Paues's printed text on pp. 227–237, including her notes, printed page breaks, manuscript folio labels, local line numbers, Trinity comparison-line milestones and typographic supplies.

entities.label: Anna C. Paues, A Newly Discovered Manuscript of the Poema Morale (1907)
entities.description: Paues's article introduces McClean 123, describes its contents, ownership, script and dialect, compares its Poema Morale copy with the six previously known versions, and prints the McClean text on pp. 227–237.

bibliographic.citation: Anna C. Paues, 'A Newly Discovered Manuscript of the Poema Morale,' Anglia 30, new series 18 (1907): 217–237.
bibliographic.title: A Newly Discovered Manuscript of the Poema Morale

entities.label: Richard Morris, Old English Homilies of the Twelfth Century, Second Series (1873)
entities.description: Morris's edition of Trinity College Cambridge MS B.14.52 includes the Poema Morale copy as A Moral Ode on printed pp. 220–232. Paues identifies this printed T text before comparing its order, readings and lineation with the new McClean copy.

bibliographic.citation: Richard Morris, ed., Old English Homilies of the Twelfth Century: From the Unique MS. B. 14. 52 in the Library of Trinity College, Cambridge, Second Series, EETS Original Series 53 (London: N. Trübner & Co., 1873), 220–232.
bibliographic.title: Old English Homilies of the Twelfth Century
bibliographic.publisher: N. Trübner & Co. for the Early English Text Society

entities.label: Cambridge, Fitzwilliam Museum, MS McClean 123
entities.description: A late-thirteenth- or circa-1300 multilingual parchment miscellany. Its final textual item is a distinctive 335-line Poema Morale version at ff. 115r–120r, preceded by two lines from Sinners Beware; f. 114v records names of English letterforms. Fifteenth-century Nuneaton ownership does not establish where the codex or poem was composed.

date_statements.label: Manuscript production
date_statements.value: Late thirteenth century or around 1300; Biblissima reports 1275–1325
date_statements.notes: This dates the physical witness, not the Poema Morale's composition.

date_statements.label: Nuneaton ownership
date_statements.notes: Ownership inscriptions associate the codex with women and the convent of Nuneaton; they do not prove production there.

holding.notes: No repository-owned exact item page was found in the current Fitzwilliam collection search; Biblissima, ARLIMA, DIMEV and the museum's published 1912 catalogue independently identify the holding and shelfmark.

physical_description.extent: 122 folios; Paues identifies ff. 121–122 as flyleaves
notes: James describes old red skin over boards, mended, with the clasp gone; Paues calls it the original Morocco-covered boards.
The text is a multilingual miscellany with French, Latin and Middle English materials; the Poema Morale copy is the last substantive item.

Paues calls the relevant scribe French. Her dialect analysis calls M Kentish with strong western admixture, while DIMEV relays LAEME localization at grid 394 213 in central Gloucestershire; these witness-language claims remain attributed and are not transferred to the abstract work.

entities.label: Trinity College Cambridge MS B.14.52
entities.description: The witness underlying Morris's 1873 printed T text, whose line order Paues cites in italics at the right of the McClean text. The current Trinity catalogue places the poem at ff. 2r–9v and the next sermon at f. 10r; Paues does not establish that she directly consulted the codex.

date_statements.label: Manuscript production
date_statements.value: Late twelfth or early thirteenth century
date_statements.notes: Hill dates this copy before 1200; the current Trinity catalogue assigns the codex to the thirteenth century.

physical_description.extent: 91 folios; 216 published digital canvases including covers, flyleaves, stubs and UV alternates

No spine, edge, head, tail, or explicit pastedown canvas is published.

entities.label: M. R. James, A Descriptive Catalogue of the McClean Collection of Manuscripts (1912)
entities.description: The Fitzwilliam Museum's published catalogue describes McClean 123 as the Nuneaton Book, its codicology and ownership, all contents, and Poema Morale at ff. 115r–120r on printed pp. 262–267.

bibliographic.citation: M. R. James, A Descriptive Catalogue of the McClean Collection of Manuscripts in the Fitzwilliam Museum (Cambridge: Cambridge University Press, 1912), no. 123, pp. 262–267.
bibliographic.title: A Descriptive Catalogue of the McClean Collection of Manuscripts in the Fitzwilliam Museum

entities.label: Betty Hill, The Twelfth-Century Conduct of Life (1977)
entities.description: A substantial modern study of the work's title, seven full witnesses, publication history, textual relationship, date, provenance, metre and homiletic content. It identifies M at McClean 123, ff. 115r–120r, and notes Paues's February 1905 transcription.

bibliographic.citation: Betty Hill, 'The Twelfth-Century Conduct of Life, Formerly the Poema Morale or A Moral Ode,' Leeds Studies in English, new series 9 (1977): 97–144.
bibliographic.title: The Twelfth-Century Conduct of Life, Formerly the Poema Morale or A Moral Ode

entities.label: Digital Index of Middle English Verse version 1.0
entities.description: Versioned CC BY 4.0 data identifying Poema Morale as DIMEV/NIMEV 1272, its seven complete witnesses, McClean 123 as wit-2113-3, Trinity B.14.52 as wit-2113-4, the separate Sinners Beware record, Paues's edition and witness-language references.

entities.label: Biblissima+ record for Cambridge, Fitzwilliam Museum, McClean 123
entities.description: A current aggregated manuscript record identifying the holding, shelfmark, broad date and onsite status. Its generic Mirador control explicitly reports that no digitized object is in the selection and exposes no item-specific image manifest.

agents.name: Anonymous scribe of the McClean 123 Poema Morale copy
notes: Paues characterizes the scribe as French; no personal identity is established.

access.access_method: Request a public Study Room appointment through the official enquiry form at least two weeks in advance and identify MS McClean 123.
access.format: Onsite consultation; non-flash research photography may be permitted subject to restrictions

access.access_method: Email the personal-study image order form to the Image Library and request one complete color digital reproduction of the whole current physical object: outer boards or covers, spine, pastedowns, all flyleaves and endleaves, every manuscript leaf including blanks and adjacent works, and head, tail and fore-edge views where offered. Identify the target separately as Poema Morale, ff. 115r–120r, and ask for a quote, file inventory, foliation crosswalk, permitted private storage terms and any separate redistribution or publication licence.

local_copies.label: Fitzwilliam personal-study image order form (2025)
notes: Supporting request document only; this is not a manuscript facsimile.

local_copies.label: Fitzwilliam Image Library terms and conditions (2024)
notes: Supporting rights document only; this is not a manuscript facsimile.

access.contact: licensing@fitzmuseum.cam.ac.uk; Image Library, The Fitzwilliam Museum, Trumpington Street, Cambridge CB2 1RB, United Kingdom; +44 (0)1223 332927
access.format: Quoted digital image delivery; form lists JPEG/TIFF supply and per-photograph fees but a complete-codex price requires an institutional quotation
notes: Requesting the whole object, rather than only ff. 115r–120r, is essential for binding, codicology, blank and adjacent-work context.
No supplied manuscript images are present in the cache. The two downloaded local copies are request and rights evidence documents, not manuscript facsimiles.
Private study supply does not permit publication, Git redistribution, print-on-demand or commercial reuse. Obtain a separate written licence for any such use.

access.access_method: Use the Biblissima and ARLIMA records for discovery, then request images from the Fitzwilliam Museum. Do not treat generic Mirador interface chrome as an item facsimile.
access.format: Metadata-only discovery records; no verified item-specific image manifest
notes: Dated negative search scope: Fitzwilliam collection search, Biblissima/Biblissima IIIF interface, ARLIMA, exact shelfmark and Nuneaton Codex web searches, and IIIF/facsimile queries on 2026-07-12.
This finding does not prove the manuscript has never been digitized or that no internal photography exists; it records only that no real complete public object could be opened.

rights.component: The medieval textual content of Poema Morale and the McClean 123 copy
rights.basis: The work and manuscript copy predate modern copyright by centuries; U-M also applies No Copyright–United States to the CME collection materials.
notes: This does not determine rights or contract conditions in modern photographs, editions, encodings or supplied reproductions.

rights.component: Paues's 1907 article, transcription and editorial contribution

rights.component: Morris's 1873 edition, transcription and editorial contribution
rights.basis: The edition was published in London in 1873 and is in the public domain in the United States.

rights.component: Google Books scan derivative distributed through Internet Archive
rights.basis: The embedded Google notice describes the book as public domain but requests personal, noncommercial use and attribution; Internet Archive item metadata gives no independent rights or licence field.

notes: The assertion is jurisdiction-specific and does not govern Fitzwilliam manuscript photography or Hill's 1977 article.

rights.component: Any manuscript images supplied by the Fitzwilliam Museum Image Library
rights.jurisdiction: Provider contract and applicable copyright law
rights.basis: The personal-study form says copyright in supplied images remains with the Fitzwilliam Museum and no supplied image may be published or reproduced without prior approval and a licence; the current Image Library terms grant only quotation-specific, non-transferable uses and require secure storage.
notes: A private-study order is not permission to commit facsimiles to Git, publish them in the viewer, sublicense them, use them in commercial print-on-demand, or redistribute them.
If the museum supplies a complete facsimile, retain it only in the ignored private cache until the exact quotation and licence are recorded.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Manuscript contractions, abbreviations, uncertain or supplied letters and words
editorial_practices.representation: Expanded letters are italicized; supplied material is bracketed; original readings appear in footnotes.
editorial_practices.description: Paues explicitly states that she expands contractions and abbreviations with supplied letters in italics, uses brackets for additions and records original readings in notes.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Manuscript proper names normally written with lowercase initials except at line beginnings
editorial_practices.representation: Paues supplies capitals for proper names.
editorial_practices.description: The edition normalizes capitalization of proper names rather than reproducing manuscript practice literally.
Paues states that the punctuation is her own.

editorial_practices.source_feature: McClean lines differ in order and extent from Trinity witness T
editorial_practices.representation: Italic right-margin numbers identify corresponding T lines; selected other witness readings appear in footnotes.
editorial_practices.description: Paues makes the McClean witness the transcribed text while using Trinity lineation as a visible concordance and comparison layer.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Twenty-one-page article with ten introductory pages followed by eleven pages of edited text
editorial_practices.description: The digital item excludes Paues's manuscript description, linguistic analysis and stemmatic discussion on pp. 217–226, retaining only the TEXT section and closing signature.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Paues prints local M line numbers and italic T concordance figures in two margin zones.
editorial_practices.representation: The XML serializes both as MILESTONE/@N, with T values suffixed '(T)', rather than preserving the page's spatial alignment.
editorial_practices.description: The encoding retains comparison-line semantics but transforms the printed two-dimensional apparatus into inline milestones. It also transcribes printed f. 115b incorrectly as 155b.
editorial_practices.scope: 216 non-folio line milestones and eleven folio milestones in the TEXT section
notes: The XML contains 148 T-suffixed milestones and 68 purely numeric local-line milestones.

evidence.citation: General Corpus pinned CME source XML, CME00070.xml.
evidence.locator: IDG/@ID, BIBNO, VID; TEXT/DIV1/LG; PB 227–237; folio and line MILESTONE elements; TEMPHEAD
evidence.summary: Primary repository evidence for the exact identifier family, selected source pages, 337 encoded lines, notes, Paues signature, source folio labels, T comparison milestones, editorial italics and 2016 review note.

evidence.citation: Structural and content audit of pinned CME00070.xml against Paues's printed text.
evidence.locator: Counts of DIV1, LG, L, NOTE, PB, MILESTONE and HI; first two and final lines; ordered folio milestones
evidence.summary: Counts one DIV1, one LG, 337 L, 36 NOTE, eleven PB, 227 MILESTONE and 38 italic HI spans. The first two L elements are Sinners Beware; 335 are the McClean Poema Morale version. Comparison with printed p. 228 verifies that XML 155b should read 115b.

evidence.citation: Digital Index of Middle English Verse, version 1.0, DIMEV/NIMEV 1272, record-2113.
evidence.locator: Records.xml record-2113/wit-2113-3/wit-2113-4 and record-5698; Manuscripts.xml CamFitzMc123/CamTCC335; Bibliography.xml Paues1907; LICENSE; README
evidence.summary: Versioned current index evidence for preferred title, incipit, 398-line couplet extent, moral-advice subject, seven complete witnesses, McClean and Trinity folios and first/last lines, the separate Sinners Beware heading, Paues edition, witness language references and CC BY 4.0 release terms.

evidence.citation: Biblissima+, Cambridge, Fitzwilliam Museum, McClean 123, Q269506.
evidence.locator: holding, date, notes, data source and Mirador selection control
evidence.summary: Current aggregate evidence for holding, shelfmark, 1275–1325 date range and onsite-only note. Direct HTML inspection finds the Mirador control title 'Aucun objet numérisé dans votre sélection' and no item-specific manifest, despite generic IIIF explanatory chrome.
notes: The page warns that its record contains contradictions inherited from bibliography and omits Poema Morale from the displayed contents; it is not treated as a complete item catalogue.

evidence.citation: ARLIMA, manuscript record for Cambridge, Fitzwilliam Museum, McClean 123.
evidence.locator: physical description, owner and bibliography
evidence.summary: Independent manuscript handlist evidence for current holding, shelfmark, late-thirteenth-century date, parchment support, 262 × 167 mm dimensions, Morocco binding, Frank McClean bequest and exact Paues article links.

evidence.citation: The Fitzwilliam Museum, Study Room.
evidence.locator: Access; Making an appointment; Photography
evidence.summary: Official route for members of the public to request an onsite appointment. The page allows non-flash research photography subject to restrictions and directs publication or reproduction applications to the Image Library.

evidence.citation: The Fitzwilliam Museum, Image Library.
evidence.locator: Contact; Placing an order; Copyright; terms
evidence.summary: Official image and reproduction enquiry route, licensing email and telephone, personal-study and high-resolution forms, quoted fees, turnaround guidance and separation between supplied images and reproduction rights.

evidence.citation: The Fitzwilliam Museum, Images for Personal Study, Reference, Research or Teaching Use, 2025 form.
evidence.locator: pp. 1–3: contact, supply fees, order details, publication restriction and new studio photography costs
evidence.summary: The current form provides email and postal request routes, says institutional affiliation is optional, lists JPEG/TIFF and new bound-book photography fees, requires a quotation and prepayment, and forbids publication or reproduction of supplied images without prior approval and a licence.

evidence.citation: The Fitzwilliam Museum Image Library Terms and Conditions, 21 May 2024.
evidence.locator: clauses 1–3, especially quotation-specific non-transferable licence, secure storage and no commercial picture archive
evidence.summary: Official contract evidence that any licence is quotation-specific, non-exclusive, non-transferable, non-sublicensable and limited by use, territory and duration; images must be stored securely and cannot be placed in a commercial picture library or archive.

evidence.citation: The Fitzwilliam Museum collection search for exact shelfmark MS McClean 123 and title Nuneaton Book.
evidence.locator: exact-shelfmark and title searches on 2026-07-12
evidence.summary: The current collection search returned unrelated records and no exact McClean 123 item or image object. This negative result is used only with the Biblissima/ARLIMA and broader IIIF/facsimile checks, not as proof that no photography exists.

evidence.citation: Trinity College Cambridge, The James Catalogue of Western Manuscripts, B.14.52.
evidence.locator: main details, contents item I, IIIF link and licence
evidence.summary: Current institutional evidence for shelfmark, James number 335, title, thirteenth-century date, parchment, 91-folio extent, dimensions, poem beginning at f. 2r, next sermon at f. 10r, exact IIIF manifest and CC BY-NC 4.0 licence.

evidence.citation: Trinity College Cambridge IIIF manifest for MS B.14.52.
evidence.locator: 216 canvases; first, target, ordinary last and UV alternate surfaces
evidence.summary: The official complete published inventory contains 216 canvases. Poema Morale maps to f. 2r–9v / canvases 13–28; the sequence includes covers, flyleaves, manuscript leaves, stubs and UV alternates.

evidence.citation: Local audit of the complete Trinity B.14.52 IIIF bundle reused for CME00070.
evidence.locator: manifest.json; inventory.json; all 216 images; canvases 1, 11, 13, 28–31, 206 and 216
evidence.summary: Reverified exact hard-link identity, 729,594,857 bytes, SHA-256, ZIP integrity, 216-file inventory, 214 exact 1800,-width requests, two native full stub requests, representative dimensions and visual boundaries. Canvas 11 is the f. 1r table; the poem begins at canvas 13 / f. 2r and ends at canvas 28 / f. 9v.

open_questions.question: Does the Fitzwilliam Museum already hold or permit creation of a complete color digital facsimile of MS McClean 123, and what exact private-storage, research, Git-publication, print-on-demand and commercial permissions would govern it?
next_steps: Submit the official personal-study image order form for the whole physical object, naming every board, spine, pastedown, endleaf, blank, manuscript leaf and edge where offered.
Map the work separately as ff. 115r–120r and ask for an image inventory and foliation crosswalk.
Request separate written terms for private cache retention, public Git distribution, noncommercial publication, commercial print-on-demand and sublicensing; do not infer any of these from delivery.

open_questions.question: How should Paues's description of the McClean copy as Kentish with strong western admixture be reconciled with DIMEV's LAEME grid 394 213 localization in central Gloucestershire?
next_steps: Inspect the exact LAEME corpus profile and its text-language versus scribal-language methodology.
Consult post-1977 dialectological work on McClean 123 before assigning a single modern dialect region.
Keep both claims at the witness layer and do not use either as proof of the abstract work's composition place.

open_questions.question: Were the two Sinners Beware lines before the McClean Poema Morale copied accidentally from an exemplar containing both works, or intentionally used as a motto?
next_steps: Inspect f. 115r and the facing f. 114v in a complete facsimile for layout, ruling, initial and spacing evidence.
Compare the two lines with the full Jesus College MS 29 Sinners Beware text and later textual scholarship.
Continue to exclude the two lines from the 335-line McClean Poema Morale count while preserving them in the encoded-source boundary.

open_questions.question: What source images, initial keying date, correction history and item-level production documentation underlie U-M CME00070 before the documented 2016 review?
next_steps: Ask U-M for item-level production metadata and the source image package used for keying.
Confirm whether the 155b folio error entered at initial keying or a later transformation.
Treat TEMPHEAD as evidence of a 2016 validation pass, not as the creation date.
